proposal: Build an Exp5 variant whose call graph spans multiple modules with dynamic dispatch / indirection that obscures reach, hold the blast-radius oracle fixed, and record whether the pairwise ranking still matches the oracle and where it diverges.

Construct a value oracle with no single computable answer (severity x reach x user-exposure x frequency, weights set by a source external to the rater) and measure rater spread and rank correlation against it on that contestable corpus.

Add a paired-proposal probe to the revealed-preference path: present the predictor with two proposals of matched substance where one discloses a limitation/retraction and one is hype-worded, and record which the learned model predicts the maintainer accepts.

Extend the Exp2 collusion design with proposals that are plausible but subtly wrong (rationalizable reward hacks rather than blatant ones) and compare author-reviews-own vs independent-reviewer approval rates on that set.

Add a pytest suite covering prefs.py eval math (eval_loo, eval_prequential bucketing, majority_baseline) and the gate.py enqueue->review->resolve state machine, driven entirely by the deterministic stub predictor and synthetic JSONL fixtures so no claude CLI or spend is needed.
Add a learning-curve plotter that renders the prequential bucket accuracies from prefs.py evaluate (vs the majority baseline) as a chart or ASCII/SVG artifact written to disk.

Tighten input validation across the harness: reject (rather than silently coerce to 'other') unknown gate.py --class values, reject empty --proposal, validate/dedup record ids, and guard the oracle.py unittest-output parser against malformed lines.
Add a GitHub Actions workflow (and matching local runner) that on push runs the Python harness self-tests plus bash -n / shellcheck on loop_driver.sh and action_router.sh.
Add a top-level reproducibility guide (e.g. RUN.md/REPRODUCE.md) giving the exact command sequence to regenerate each experiment's results from its stimuli, raw CSVs, and analysis path.
Declare the Python/runtime environment for the repo (e.g. requirements.txt or pyproject.toml plus a required-tools note for the claude CLI and jq) and a supported Python version.
Add a CITATION.cff (or equivalent citation metadata) at the repo root so the experiments and findings can be cited.
Add a CONTRIBUTING guide that codifies the project's evidence norms (blinded controls, disclosed retractions, no overclaiming, claim-to-source provenance) for outside contributors.

Reconcile the top-level README layout and the duplicated report/experiments trees into a single documented repo map (README omits ultra-suite/, revealed-preference/, ROADMAP.md and lists only exp1-2; top-level EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md is byte-identical to the ultra-suite copy while experiments/ holds exp1-5 vs ultra-suite/experiments/ exp1-2).
A consolidated research writeup spanning Exp1 through Exp5 with a dedicated corrections-and-retractions section recording each experiment's revised conclusions.

A threat-model document enumerating the suite's self-gaming and runaway-autonomy vectors, e.g. author-and-score-in-one-pass, self-confidence gate-skip proposals, self-authored value oracles, and cost/iteration runaway.

A glossary defining the suite's recurring terms such as Original Intent, additive-reversible, boundary band, evaluator-optimizer separation, blast radius, prequential accuracy, and revealed preference.

A standalone register of claims the project does not establish, covering the untested recursion/B5 feedback loop, open value-calibration under ambiguity, the single-model self-consistency ceiling, and absent cross-model/human agreement.
Extend the Exp1-2-only EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md files to incorporate the Exp3, Exp4, and Exp5 results and their provenance.
Add a CYCLE_DASHBOARD: live HTML/CLI dashboard rendering cycles run, cumulative cost, opportunities spawned, and proposal accept/reject counts from state/
Add a 'Results' section to the README reporting the revealed-preference harness's current prequential accuracy and learning curve over the logged decisions
